mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.64% provides room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS (month-over-month growth > 0.2%) signals robust labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained demand growth.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive PAYEMS signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining RSAFS (month-over-month contraction > 1%) signals weakening consumer demand that portends lower corporate revenues and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative RSAFS signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO (month-over-month contraction > 0.3%) combined with rising UNRATE (increase > 0.2pp in 1 month) signals supply-side deterioration and labor market softening, indicating recessionary pressure that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (month-over-month growth > 2%) signals strengthening housing demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved sentiment and near-term equity inflows.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST spikes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remains above 2.5%, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE (increase > 0.3pp in 3 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer purchasing power and corporate hiring, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through demand destruction.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 (quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% annualized) and RSAFS (month-over-month growth > 0.5%) signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24065.956 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 733537 million dollars
regime_hint: strong cyclical expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
